summary: The authors introduce BatMIL, a WSI classification framework that utilizes a hybrid hyperbolic-Euclidean representation, an S4 backbone for long-range dependencies, and a chunk-level MoE module to outperform state-of-the-art MIL approaches.

blocks.content: Geometry-Aware State Space Model: A New Paradigm for Whole-Slide Image Representation

source_span.quote: # Geometry-Aware State Space Model: A New Paradigm for Whole-Slide Image Representation

blocks.content: Accurate analysis of histopathological images is critical for disease diagnosis and treatment planning.

source_span.quote: Accurate analysis of histopathological images is critical for disease diagnosis and treatment planning.

blocks.content: most existing methods implicitly embed patch representations in homogeneous Euclidean spaces, overlooking the hierarchical organization and regional heterogeneity of pathological tissues.

source_span.quote: However, most existing methods implicitly embed patch representations in homogeneous Euclidean spaces, overlooking the hierarchical organization and regional heterogeneity of pathological tissues.

blocks.content: we introduce a hybrid hyperbolic-Euclidean representation that embeds WSI features in dual geometric spaces, enabling complementary modeling of hierarchical tissue structures and local morphological details.

source_span.quote: To address this limitation, we introduce a hybrid hyperbolic-Euclidean representation that embeds WSI features in dual geometric spaces, enabling complementary modeling of hierarchical tissue structures and local morphological details.

blocks.content: Building on this formulation, we develop BatMIL, a WSI classification framework that leverages both geometric spaces.

source_span.quote: Building on this formulation, we develop BatMIL, a WSI classification framework that leverages both geometric spaces.

blocks.content: To model long-range dependencies among thousands of patches, we employ a structured state space sequence model (S4) backbone that encodes patch sequences with linear computational complexity.

source_span.quote: To model long-range dependencies among thousands of patches, we employ a structured state space sequence model (S4) backbone that encodes patch sequences with linear computational complexity.

blocks.content: to account for regional heterogeneity, we introduce a chunk-level mixture-of-experts (MoE) module that groups patches into regions and dynamically routes them to specialized subnetworks

source_span.quote: Furthermore, to account for regional heterogeneity, we introduce a chunk-level mixture-of-experts (MoE) module that groups patches into regions and dynamically routes them to specialized subnetworks, improving representational capacity while reducing redundant computation.

blocks.content: Extensive experiments on seven WSI datasets spanning six cancer types demonstrate that BatMIL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art MIL approaches in slide-level classification tasks.

source_span.quote: Extensive experiments on seven WSI datasets spanning six cancer types demonstrate that BatMIL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art MIL approaches in slide-level classification tasks.

blocks.content: geometry-aware representation learning offers a promising direction for next-generation computational pathology.

source_span.quote: These results indicate that geometry-aware representation learning offers a promising direction for next-generation computational pathology.
